title: Ramp Travel API
summary: Ramp Travel API interview drills adapted from the MIT-licensed keroshanpillay/ramp-interview project: pagination, auth, retries, asyncio, outbox sync, joins, and spend rollups.
guideBody: These problems all run against an in-process fake travel API. There is no real network, no external service, and no setup. The fake service is reset before every visible or hidden test.
guideBullets: Synchronous HTTP problems use `import requests`; the fake `requests.get` and `requests.post` return objects with `.status_code`, `.headers`, `.json()`, `.text`, and `.raise_for_status()`.
Async problems use `import travel_api`; its methods are awaitable and intentionally yield control so ordering and concurrency bugs show up without real sleeping.
Always use the `base_url` and `token` parameters passed into the function. Hidden tests use different tokens.
Problem prompts describe only the endpoint or async method needed for that exercise; the shared fake API behavior is the same across the module.

sections.label: Resilience & Debugging
sections.blurb: Rate limits, flaky endpoints, and broken integration code

sections.label: Asyncio Workflows
sections.blurb: Fan-out, bounded concurrency, and async aggregation

sections.label: Writes & Outbox Sync
sections.blurb: Stable client request ids and idempotent write semantics

sections.label: Matching & Reconciliation
sections.blurb: Hash joins, date windows, and messy finance data
